chapter_title.en: Chapter 133 – The Saltwort (a salt-water plant), and the Cyperus

text: عَنْهُ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي وَلَّادٍ قَالَ رَأَيْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي الْحِجْرِ وَ هُوَ قَاعِدٌ وَ مَعَهُ عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ فَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ ضُرِبَتْ عَلَيَّ أَسْنَانِي فَأَخَذْتُ السُّعْدَ فَدَلَكْتُ بِهِ أَسْنَانِي فَنَفَعَنِي ذَلِكَ وَ سَكَنَتْ عَنِّي .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Wallad who said,

‘I saw Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> the 1<sup>st</sup> in Al Hijr (at the Kabah), and he<sup>asws</sup> was seated, and with him<sup>asws</sup> were a number of his<sup>asws</sup> family members, so I hear him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘My teeth hurt, so I took the cyperus and rubbed it down by my teeth, and that benefitted me, and settled down from it’.
